title: Firmware-wide location provider abstraction
description: Why this exists: location/GNSS is firmware-wide, not specific to LilyGO boards and not necessarily tied to an app or app UI. Any LICHEN firmware target may have integrated GNSS, external GNSS attached to the board, no local GNSS, network-derived location, or location injected through Meshtastic, MeshCore, KISS, gpsd, or native LICHEN app interfaces. Firmware needs one source-agnostic location service so board drivers, network services, UI, CoAP resources, beacons, position sharing, and app compatibility layers do not each invent their own GNSS assumptions. What needs to be done: define and implement a firmware-wide location provider abstraction with source identification, source class (onboard hardware, external board hardware, network, local app/client, manual/static), freshness/age, fix status, accuracy/quality metadata, altitude when available, trust/override policy, and conflict handling across hardware, network, and app-provided sources. Hardware and network sources must feed this provider directly; app interfaces may feed or consume it but do not own it. Acceptance criteria: supported location source classes are enumerated; precedence/override policy is documented; stale/fresh semantics are tested; hardware-derived, network-derived, static/manual, and app-injected locations use the same internal representation; UI consumes only the unified provider state; CoAP/status resources expose source class, source name, fix state, and age; two code-review passes are completed before closure.
notes:
Related but separate from project-LICHEN-2067 date/time provider: GNSS/gpsd may supply both position and time, but firmware should model location fix/source independently from wall-clock validity/accuracy/source.
2026-07-01 no-hardware core provider slice: implemented firmware-wide HAL location provider core in lichen/subsys/lichen/hal. Added source classes NONE/ONBOARD_HARDWARE/EXTERNAL_HARDWARE/NETWORK/LOCAL_CLIENT/MANUAL_STATIC, fix states NONE/NO_FIX/2D/3D/STALE/ERROR, source_name, age, horizontal/vertical accuracy fields, lichen_hal_location_sample, lichen_hal_location_submit(), lichen_hal_location_clear(), and ZTEST uptime hooks. Provider keeps one sample per source class, copies/truncates source_name, selects highest-priority fresh usable fix first, then fresh metadata, then stale metadata, with priority MANUAL_STATIC > LOCAL_CLIENT > NETWORK > EXTERNAL_HARDWARE > ONBOARD_HARDWARE. Freshness configured by CONFIG_LICHEN_LOCATION_FRESHNESS_MAX_AGE_S default 300s. Stale snapshots retain source/fix/age metadata but suppress position, time, altitude, satellites, accuracy, and fix_source_valid. Validation rejects invalid enums, GNSS fix_source on non-hardware sources, partial coordinates, 2D/3D fixes without both coordinates, negative observed_uptime_ms, and clamps future observed_uptime_ms to current uptime; extreme accepted ages saturate at UINT32_MAX. Legacy GNSS fallback now exposes source_class ONBOARD_HARDWARE, source_name gnss0, fix_state NO_FIX, and leaves fix_source_valid false for no-fix consistency.

Tests added/updated in lichen/tests/hal cover fresh GNSS fix metadata, stale suppression, partial/no-coordinate fix rejection, source precedence and stale fallback, no-fix metadata not blocking lower-priority usable fix, fresh no-fix metadata beating stale high-priority metadata, invalid fix_source, negative/future observed uptime, extreme age saturation, and suite cleanup. Review evidence: first review passes found P2 gaps around stale metadata selection, source class/fix_source constraints, status exposure, timestamp overflow, coordinate-less 2D/3D fixes, and fallback fix_source_valid consistency. Fixed core P2s in code/tests and split remaining acceptance into children. Final review agents Aristotle the 5th and Meitner the 5th found no P0/P1/P2 issues and verified prior P2 fixes. Remaining acceptance is intentionally represented by blockers project-LICHEN-zte2.1 gateway /status CBOR exposure, zte2.2 app-interface/UI mirroring, zte2.3 puck GNSS submission, zte2.4 local-client injected locations, and zte2.5 network/static/manual producers. Parent remains in_progress and blocked by those children.

Validation on EC2 builder i-05e27e074db786e22 in us-east-2a with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr, clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-location-v1: git diff --check PASS locally. HAL Twister after final fixes: west twister -T lichen/tests/hal -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-location-hal-v8 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen PASS, 4/4 configurations, 64 executed cases, 4 skipped, 0 warnings, 79.58s. Consumer regression Twister: west twister -T lichen/tests/app_interface -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-location-consumers-v4 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen PASS, 3/5 configurations passed, 2 static skips, 67 executed cases, 35 skipped, 0 warnings, 67.26s. Earlier failing HAL v4-v6/v5 outputs were fixture bugs corrected before final passing v7/v8; final evidence is v8 plus consumers v4.
close_reason: All 9 children closed. HAL location provider implemented with source classes, fix states, freshness/staleness, precedence policy. Tests pass: HAL 64 cases, consumers 67 cases on native_sim/qemu_x86. Gateway /status exposes unified location metadata.
